content:
🌟 mercur | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Building and managing custom multi-vendor e-commerce marketplaces.
✨ Key Features: • Block-based architecture for reusable modules and extensions • Standalone React-based vendor portal • Dashboard SDK with Vite plugin and file-based routing • CLI for project scaffolding and management • Full code ownership without black-box dependencies
📖 Summary: Mercur is an open-source, block-based marketplace platform built on MedusaJS, designed for creating custom B2B and B2C marketplaces. It offers full code ownership, a modern tech stack, and a vendor portal, empowering businesses with flexibility and control.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 twake-drive | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide an open-source, self-hosted file management and cloud storage solution as an alternative to proprietary services like Google Drive.
✨ Key Features: • File tree visualization • File and folder upload • File and folder sharing via URLs • File and folder search functionality
📖 Summary: Twake Drive is an open-source web application that functions as an alternative to Google Drive, providing robust file management capabilities. It allows users to organize, upload, share files and folders via URLs, and search their content, all within a user-friendly interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 paperbanana | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automated generation of publication-quality academic diagrams, statistical plots, and research visuals from text descriptions.
✨ Key Features: • Agentic framework for generating academic diagrams and plots • Supports OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and Google Gemini providers • Two-phase multi-agent pipeline with iterative refinement • Multiple VLM and image generation providers • Input optimization layer
📖 Summary: PaperBanana is an open-source, community-driven implementation for automating the generation of academic illustrations, diagrams, and statistical plots from text descriptions. It extends the original PaperBanana concept with new domains like slide generation and offers a flexible, agentic framework supporting multiple LLM and VLM providers.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 tiny-gpu | SystemVerilog
🎯 Primary Use Case: Educational tool for learning GPU hardware architecture and parallel processing from the ground up.
✨ Key Features: • Minimal GPU design in Verilog • Fully documented code (<15 files) • Detailed documentation on architecture & ISA • Working matrix addition/multiplication kernels • Support for kernel simulation & execution traces
📖 Summary: tiny-gpu is a minimal, documented Verilog implementation of a GPU designed to teach hardware-level GPU architecture and parallelization principles. It focuses on core GPGPU concepts rather than graphics-specific features, offering a simplified model for learning.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ ChatDev | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to rapidly build and execute customized multi-agent systems for various development tasks without writing code.
✨ Key Features: • Zero-Code Multi-Agent Platform • Customizable Agent Configuration • Workflow Definition • Automated Software Development Lifecycle (ChatDev 1.0) • Multi-Agent Orchestration
📖 Summary: ChatDev 2.0 (DevAll) is a zero-code multi-agent platform for developing diverse applications by configuring agents and workflows. It evolved from ChatDev 1.0, a virtual software company automating the software development lifecycle through intelligent agent collaboration.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
